ksa_text: Knowledge of current frontiers in machine learning and AI research, including recent advances in foundation models, reinforcement learning, causal ML, and AI safety research.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of basic system administration, network, and operating system hardening techniques.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of basic system administration, network, and operating system hardening techniques.
Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain AI/ML Engineering KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.384) to justify inclusion for the VP Data Platform. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world VP Data Platform roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm AI/ML Engineering is not a standard competency expectation for VP Data Platform. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the VP Data Platform competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.384) and edge-domain placement (AI/ML Engineering) do not meet inclusion threshold for the VP Data Platform. All passes support exclusion.
